prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `9f5f939b`.
codex_response:
Rule summary: the output marks each `8` cell that is the center of a dashed plus made from four separate `1x2` dominoes: two horizontal bars in the same row and two vertical bars in the same column.

I discarded the Sonnet hint and used the Opus hint as the starting hypothesis. Sonnet describes a generic row/column intersection rule that fails on training pair 3; the corrected object-level reading is recorded in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_9f5f939b/notes.md#L1). No new primitive was needed, so `arc2/dsl_ext.py` was left unchanged.

The task package is in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_9f5f939b/__init__.py#L1), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_9f5f939b/verifier.py#L4), and [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_9f5f939b/generator.py#L103). The verifier detects centers by intersecting left/right midpoint proposals from horizontal size-2 objects with top/bottom midpoint proposals from vertical size-2 objects. The generator explicitly places 1-3 valid dashed-plus motifs, adds sparse non-touching domino distractors, and biases sizes toward the observed family (`16x16`, `20x20`, occasional `8x8`).

Validation passed end to end: official examples `4/4`, generated examples `1000/1000`. I also opened `preview_01.png` via a terminal thumbnail render and it looked consistent with the official sparse-domino distribution.
